title: Seismic structure and tectonics of the southern Arauco Basin, south-central Chile (~38°S)
abstract: The kinematic evolution of the Arauco Basin is studied using high-resolution seismic reflection data across the southern Arauco forearc basin. Three structural domains are identified: inversion, extension, and accretion. Seven syn-kinematic sequences are recognized overlying the Late Carboniferous to Triassic basement. Shortening began in the southern Arauco Basin around 34 Ma, coeval with a major readjustment of the plate convergence rate, and a marked erosional unconformity (34–23 Ma) represents subsequent uplift during the Oligocene, followed by a contractional Miocene deformation phase.

location.description: Southern Arauco forearc basin offshore south-central Chile (~38°S); basin architecture and kinematic evolution

data.name: High-Resolution MCS Profiles — Southern Arauco Basin ~38°S
data.description: High-resolution MCS reflection profiles in the southern Arauco forearc basin (~38°S); seven seismic sequences identified documenting 34 Ma basin inversion history; three structural domains (inversion, extension, accretion). [SPOC cruise (SO161, 2001) datasets searchable on PANGAEA]

_data_note: Tectonophysics paper; behind paywall. Uses SPOC (SO161) or related GEOMAR MCS data accessible via MGDS.

processing.summary: High-resolution MCS reflection data from the southern Arauco forearc basin (~38°S) were processed and interpreted for seismic stratigraphic analysis. Seven syn-kinematic seismic sequences above Late Carboniferous to Triassic basement reveal a complex basin evolution including shortening at 34 Ma, erosional unconformity (34–23 Ma), and Miocene contractional deformation. Three structural domains are mapped.
workflow: 1. MCS data processing (demux, filter, NMO, stack)
processing.notes: Seismic stratigraphic and structural analysis of a forearc basin
